expectations.text: Creates a git worktree on a dedicated branch before making any changes
expectations.evidence: SKILL.md step 1 explicitly instructs worktree creation with timestamped branch name before any other action

expectations.text: Runs the appropriate audit command (npm audit, yarn audit, pnpm audit) for the detected package manager
expectations.evidence: audit-workflow.md instructs running $PM audit --json for each discovered package.json directory

expectations.text: Does NOT run npm/yarn/pnpm install before the audit (audit reads from lock files)
expectations.evidence: SKILL.md step 6 and audit-workflow.md both explicitly note: skip install for security audit workflows

expectations.text: Applies Critical+High severity filter by default when user hasn't specified one
expectations.evidence: audit-workflow.md states: 'If no severity preference was expressed, default to Critical + High'

expectations.text: Removes the worktree at the end, even if no vulnerabilities were found or no PR was created
expectations.evidence: SKILL.md step 10 says 'Always run cleanup, even if the skill fails mid-run'

expectations.text: Creates a git worktree on a dedicated branch before making any changes
expectations.evidence: Without skill guidance, model typically uses git checkout -b directly in the working directory rather than a worktree

expectations.text: Runs the appropriate audit command (npm audit, yarn audit, pnpm audit) for the detected package manager
expectations.evidence: Running npm audit is common knowledge and Claude would detect the package manager from lock files

expectations.text: Does NOT run npm/yarn/pnpm install before the audit (audit reads from lock files)
expectations.evidence: Without explicit instruction, model often runs npm install first as a precaution before any dependency operation

expectations.text: Applies Critical+High severity filter by default when user hasn't specified one
expectations.evidence: Without skill guidance, model would likely fix all severities or ask user rather than defaulting to Critical+High

expectations.text: Removes the worktree at the end, even if no vulnerabilities were found or no PR was created
expectations.evidence: Without worktree, no cleanup step needed — any branch created in-place would remain (passes vacuously)

notes: Third assertion passes vacuously — no worktree created without skill, so cleanup is a non-issue

expectations.text: Displays the js-deps help summary including supported package managers and workflow descriptions
expectations.evidence: SKILL.md routes --help to references/interactive-help.md which defines the exact help summary text to display

expectations.text: Presents the workflow selection question (Update dependencies vs Security fixes only) via AskUserQuestion
expectations.evidence: interactive-help.md Question 1 instructs using AskUserQuestion with multiSelect: false presenting these two workflow options

expectations.text: Does not create a worktree or start any workflow before the user answers
expectations.evidence: interactive-help.md says to display help and ask questions first; worktree creation (SKILL.md step 1) only begins after workflow selection and question flow completes

expectations.text: Displays the js-deps help summary including supported package managers and workflow descriptions
expectations.evidence: Without skill context, model doesn't know what 'js-deps' is or what help summary to display — would likely say it's an unknown command

expectations.text: Presents the workflow selection question (Update dependencies vs Security fixes only) via AskUserQuestion
expectations.evidence: Without skill definition, model has no structured interactive help flow to present; would handle the prompt generically

expectations.text: Does not create a worktree or start any workflow before the user answers
expectations.evidence: Without skill context, model would not start any workflow — it simply doesn't know what js-deps is

notes: Third assertion passes trivially — without skill knowledge, no workflow would be initiated at all

expectations.evidence: SKILL.md step 1 creates worktree before all other steps

expectations.evidence: update-workflow.md maps 'latest' phrasing to Patch+Minor+Major; interactive-help.md defines this as the all-versions tier

expectations.text: Runs build/lint/test validation after updating packages
expectations.evidence: SKILL.md step 7 instructs running available build/lint/test scripts per directory after each update

expectations.text: Commit message notes major version changes (e.g. 'chore: update dependencies (major: pkg vX->Y)')
expectations.evidence: update-workflow.md On Success section specifies: 'Major bumps included: chore: update dependencies (major: pkg1 vX->Y, pkg2 vX->Y)'

expectations.text: Creates a git worktree on a dedicated branch
expectations.evidence: Without skill guidance, model would use git checkout -b in the current working directory rather than an isolated worktree

expectations.text: Runs npm/yarn/pnpm install before running the outdated check
expectations.evidence: Running npm install before npm outdated is common practice Claude would likely follow

expectations.text: Applies Patch+Minor+Major filter (includes all updates) because user said 'latest'
expectations.evidence: Without skill's filter model, user's 'latest' is ambiguous — model might update patch+minor by default or ask rather than apply full major filter

expectations.text: Runs build/lint/test validation after updating packages
expectations.evidence: Running build/test after updates is standard practice Claude would likely do

expectations.text: Commit message notes major version changes (e.g. 'chore: update dependencies (major: pkg vX->Y)')
expectations.evidence: Without skill's commit message spec, model would use a generic message like 'chore: update dependencies' without the structured major-bump notation

expectations.text: Creates a git worktree on a dedicated branch
expectations.evidence: SKILL.md step 1 creates worktree regardless of argument scope

expectations.text: Only updates react, react-dom, and @types/react — not all packages
expectations.evidence: SKILL.md step 5 parses $ARGUMENTS to determine target packages; skill constrains updates to only the named packages

expectations.text: Only installs dependencies in directories where at least one of the named packages appears
expectations.evidence: SKILL.md step 6: 'If specific packages were identified in step 5 (not "."), only install in directories where those packages appear'

expectations.text: Asks the user about version scope (patch/minor/major) via AskUserQuestion since no scope was specified
expectations.evidence: interactive-help.md Question 2a is presented when workflow is 'Update dependencies' and no scope inline — skill's interactive flow covers this case

expectations.text: Creates a git worktree on a dedicated branch
expectations.evidence: Without skill guidance, model would not use git worktree; would operate directly on working directory

expectations.text: Only updates react, react-dom, and @types/react — not all packages
expectations.evidence: With explicit package names in the prompt, model would naturally scope the update to those packages

expectations.text: Only installs dependencies in directories where at least one of the named packages appears
expectations.evidence: Without skill's directory-filtering logic, model would likely run npm install in all directories or in the root only

expectations.text: Asks the user about version scope (patch/minor/major) via AskUserQuestion since no scope was specified
expectations.evidence: Without skill's interactive help flow, model would likely default to latest/major without asking or use its own judgment rather than the structured AskUserQuestion flow

expectations.text: Creates a git worktree on a dedicated branch
expectations.evidence: SKILL.md step 1 creates worktree before any action

expectations.text: Applies Critical-only severity filter without asking — user specified 'critical' inline
expectations.evidence: audit-workflow.md: 'If the user specified a severity scope... inline in their request, apply it now'; interactive-help.md maps 'critical only' to Critical tier

expectations.text: Does not attempt to fix High, Moderate, or Low vulnerabilities
expectations.evidence: Severity filter table in audit-workflow.md: Critical only = only Critical severities. Skill applies this filter before the update step

expectations.text: Does not run npm install before auditing
expectations.evidence: SKILL.md step 6 explicitly skips install for security audit workflows; audit-workflow.md reinforces this

expectations.text: Creates a git worktree on a dedicated branch
expectations.evidence: Without skill, model operates in-place without isolated worktree

expectations.text: Applies Critical-only severity filter without asking — user specified 'critical' inline
expectations.evidence: User's prompt explicitly says 'critical' — model would reasonably interpret this as a filter for critical-only vulnerabilities

expectations.text: Does not attempt to fix High, Moderate, or Low vulnerabilities
expectations.evidence: With 'critical only' in the prompt, model would scope its fixes accordingly

expectations.text: Does not run npm install before auditing
expectations.evidence: Without explicit skill instruction, model often runs npm install as a precautionary step before any npm operation

notes: Worktree isolation assertions (eval 1, 3, 4, 5): with_skill 4/4 pass, without_skill 0/4 pass — the skill's worktree step is the single biggest differentiator; Claude never uses git worktree without explicit instruction.

Install-skip-for-audit assertions (eval 1, 5): with_skill 2/2 pass, without_skill 0/2 pass — the explicit 'skip install for audit' note in both SKILL.md and audit-workflow.md prevents a common over-cautious behavior.
Default severity filter (eval 1): with_skill passes, without_skill fails — the Critical+High default is skill-specific knowledge; without the skill, model either fixes everything or prompts the user.
Eval 2 (--help flag) shows the largest delta: with_skill 3/3, without_skill 1/3 — two assertions pass trivially without skill because no workflow starts without knowing what js-deps is.
Prompt-derived filters (eval 5 severity, eval 3 'latest' interpretation): without_skill gets credit when the user's phrasing is explicit enough; the skill matters most for defaults and structure.
The skill adds ~51s and ~11k tokens overhead per run from reading SKILL.md and reference files; this is the expected cost of the structured multi-step workflow.
High token variance in with_skill runs (stddev 8086) reflects workflow complexity differences: help flow (eval 2) is brief; full update workflows (eval 3) are long.
Assertion 'Does not create worktree or start workflow before user answers' (eval 2) passes trivially in without_skill — worth revisiting in a future iteration to make it more discriminating.
